description_long: This object is a stylized flower with broad, overlapping petals arranged in a radial pattern around a central disk. The petals are pale peach with subtle yellow gradients, giving a soft and delicate appearance. The central disk is slightly raised and textured, resembling the reproductive structure of a real flower. The petals are thick and somewhat angular, with visible layering and some curling at the edges. There is no visible stem or leaves attached to the flower, and the underside shows a rounded, bulbous base. The overall form is organic and naturalistic, suitable for decorative or landscaping use in urban environments such as parks or gardens.
description: A pale peach flower with broad, overlapping petals radiating from a textured central disk. The petals are thick, layered, and softly colored with hints of yellow.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full radial arrangement of the petals and the central disk.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the thickness and layering of the petals, with some curling visible.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the rounded, bulbous base and the petal layering from below.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the petal overlap and the depth of the central disk.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration around the central disk, with visible overlapping and some layering, creating a symmetrical and circular floral structure.
